description:
The <tt>cmdport</tt> option in <tt>/etc/chrony.conf</tt> can be set to
<tt>0</tt> to stop chrony daemon from listening on the UDP port 323
for management connections made by chronyc.
rationale:
Minimizing the exposure of the server functionality of the chrony
daemon diminishes the attack surface.
ocil_clause: the "cmdport" option is not set to "0", is commented out, or is missing
ocil:
Verify Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 disables network management of the chrony daemon with the following command: 
$ grep -w cmdport /etc/chrony.conf
cmdport 0
fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to disable network management of the chrony daemon by adding/modifying the following line in the /etc/chrony.conf file:

cmdport 0
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must disable network management of the chrony daemon.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must disable network management of the chrony daemon.
stig.vuldiscussion: Not exposing the management interface of the chrony daemon on the network diminishes the attack space.
stig.checktext:
Verify Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 disables network management of the chrony daemon with the following command:

$ grep -w cmdport /etc/chrony.conf

cmdport 0

If the "cmdport" option is not set to "0", is commented out, or is missing, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to disable network management of the chrony daemon by adding/modifying the following line in the /etc/chrony.conf file:

cmdport 0

title: Disable network management of chrony daemon
